title: Country Manager Job Opportunity 2026 at the OPEC Fund for International Development: Lead Public Sector Projects Globally
summary: The OPEC Fund for International Development is currently accepting applications for the Country Manager (Multiple Positions) role within its Public Sector Operations department. This is a high-impact international career opportunity for professionals seeking to lead development projects, shape national strategies, and contribute to sustainable economic growth across multiple countries. Based on organizational needs, this role offers a two-year fixed-term contract with the possibility of renewal , providing candidates with a platform to engage in global development finance and policy implementation. About the OPEC Fund for International Development The OPEC Fund for International Development is a multilateral development finance institution established to promote international cooperation and support sustainable development, particularly in low- and middle-income countries. It works closely with governments, financial institutions, and development partners to finance impactful projects in infrastructure, energy, education, and more. Job Overview The Country Manager plays a central leadership role in overseeing public sector operations across assigned portfolio countries. Working closely with the Director, the successful candidate will manage the full project lifecycle—from identification and design to implementation and evaluation—while strengthening partnerships with governments and stakeholders. This role is ideal for professionals who combine strategic thinking, financial expertise, and strong stakeholder engagement skills. Contract Details Location: Austria (AT) Contract Type: Fixed-term Duration: Two years (renewable) Application Deadline: May 13, 2026 Language Requirement: English (additional languages are an asset) Key Responsibilities Policy Development and Strategic Planning The Country Manager is responsible for shaping country-level strategies and ensuring alignment with regional goals. This includes: Conducting regular assessments of portfolio countries Developing short- and medium-term strategies (3-year plans) Preparing country partnership strategies and business plans Coordinating implementation of annual work plans Project Development and Management A major component of the role involves overseeing the full project cycle: Identifying and developing a pipeline of public sector projects Evaluating financing opportunities and conducting risk assessments Leading project appraisals, including technical, economic, and ESG analysis Coordinating approvals with internal committees and governing bodies Supporting implementation, procurement, and disbursement processes The Country Manager ensures that projects are delivered efficiently and achieve their intended development impact. Stakeholder Engagement and Partnerships The role requires strong relationship-building skills to: Maintain partnerships with governments and executing agencies Collaborate with development finance institutions (DFIs) Identify co-financing opportunities Expand strategic partnerships across portfolio countries Capacity Building and Knowledge Sharing The Country Manager contributes to institutional development by: Supporting training and capacity-building initiatives Participating in knowledge-sharing activities Supervising analysts and interns Managing data systems such as MIS/SAP/EDMS Required Qualifications Applicants must meet the following criteria: Master’s degree in Economics, Finance, Engineering, or a related field At least five years of relevant professional experience Preferably two years in an international development institution Fluency in English Additional languages (Arabic, French, Portuguese, Spanish) are advantageous Core Competencies Successful candidates will demonstrate: Strong collaboration and teamwork skills Effective communication across diverse stakeholders Results-oriented mindset with strategic planning ability Adaptability and continuous learning Stakeholder-focused approach to achieving sustainable impact High levels of accountability and ethical integrity Why This Role Matters This position offers a unique opportunity to: Influence national and regional development strategies Lead high-impact public sector projects Work with international partners and governments Contribute to sustainable development goals globally Professionals in this role will play a key part in deploying financial resources effectively and ensuring that development initiatives create long-term, meaningful impact. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply before the deadline by submitting their application through the official recruitment channels of the OPEC Fund for International Development . Ensure your application highlights: Relevant project and policy experience Leadership in development initiatives Ability to manage complex, multi-stakeholder environments Final Thoughts The Country Manager position at the OPEC Fund is more than a job—it is a leadership opportunity to drive development change on a global scale. With exposure to high-level decision-making, international partnerships, and impactful projects, this role is ideal for professionals seeking to advance their careers in international development and public sector leadership. title: ERIA School of Government Fellowship 2026 in Jakarta: Fully Funded Policy Fellowship for Global Experts
summary: The Economic Research Institute for ASEAN and East Asia (ERIA) has officially opened applications for the ERIA School of Government (SOG) Fellowship 2026 , a prestigious international program designed for policy professionals, researchers, and academics aiming to shape the future of governance in Southeast Asia and East Asia. Hosted in Jakarta, Indonesia, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to engage directly with policymakers, collaborate with leading experts, and contribute to impactful regional public policy research. About the ERIA School of Government The ERIA School of Government (SOG) was established with a clear mission: to become Southeast Asia’s leading hub for public policy education, research, and leadership development. Through its fellowship programs, SOG fosters a vibrant intellectual community where scholars and practitioners collaborate on addressing complex regional challenges. By working closely with the ASEAN Secretariat , the school plays a critical role in advancing governance excellence and strengthening institutional capacity across ASEAN member states. It also promotes knowledge exchange, elevating Southeast Asia’s voice in global policy discussions. Fellowship Overview The ERIA SOG Fellowship is structured to support advanced research and policy innovation. Selected fellows will be based in Jakarta, where they will collaborate with ERIA researchers and regional stakeholders. Duration The fellowship duration ranges from 3 to 11 months , taking place between: October 1, 2026 and August 31, 2027 Key Responsibilities Fellows are expected to: Commit fully to the fellowship on a full-time basis Deliver an introductory presentation and a final public presentation Publish one policy paper in English based on their research Participate in internal meetings, seminars, and ERIA programs Contribute actively to the ERIA intellectual community Financial Benefits The fellowship provides a competitive monthly allowance of up to: USD 5,000 per month (pro-rated based on residency duration) This allowance supports research activities and living expenses during the fellowship period. However, fellows are responsible for: Accommodation Health insurance Personal expenses Research and Networking Opportunities Fellows will gain access to: ERIA’s extensive research resources A regional network of policymakers and institutions Opportunities to collaborate with ASEAN stakeholders This environment enables fellows to contribute meaningfully to regional policy development while expanding their professional network. Facilities and Work Environment Fellows will primarily work at ERIA’s offices in central Jakarta, including: The ERIA Annex Office The ERIA Headquarters within the ASEAN Secretariat Facilities include: Office workspace and laptops Conference rooms and research library Access to public lectures, executive roundtables, and policy events This immersive setting offers direct exposure to ASEAN’s policymaking ecosystem. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet one of the following: Hold a doctoral degree (for academic applicants), or Have at least 10 years of experience as a government official Additionally, candidates must: Demonstrate strong English proficiency Have policy-relevant experience Submit a research proposal addressing regional issues in ASEAN or East Asia Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Completed application form CV (maximum three pages) Research proposal (maximum five pages) Two recommendation letters Supporting documents depending on background (academic or professional) All materials must be submitted in English. Selection Process The selection process includes two stages: Stage 1: Document Screening Applications are evaluated based on: Academic and professional background Quality, originality, and relevance of research proposal Stage 2: Interview Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an interview to: Discuss their research Demonstrate expertise and motivation Show alignment with ERIA’s mission Important Dates Application Deadline: 14 May 2026 Shortlisting Interviews: 25–26 May 2026 Results Announcement: June 2026 Fellowship Start Date: 1 October 2026 Applicants must ensure that recommendation letters are submitted before the deadline to avoid disqualification. Why You Should Apply The ERIA School of Government Fellowship is more than a research opportunity—it is a platform to influence regional policy, collaborate with leading experts, and contribute to ASEAN’s future. Whether you are an academic or a seasoned professional, this fellowship allows you to: Advance your research career Gain international exposure Shape impactful policy solutions For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post ERIA School of Government Fellowship 2026 in Jakarta: Fully Funded Policy Fellowship for Global Experts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programme 2026: Launch Your Career in Software Development and Data Engineering
summary: The AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programme, supported by DEG Impulse , is a comprehensive and structured career pathway designed to equip aspiring tech professionals with the skills, experience, and global exposure needed to thrive in today’s competitive digital economy. This programme is more than just training—it is a career launchpad that combines technical upskilling, mentorship, and real-world project experience , preparing participants for international standards and full-time employment opportunities in the tech industry. What is the AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programme? The AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programme is a multi-level professional training initiative focused on developing talent in software development and data engineering. It is structured into three progressive levels , allowing participants to grow from foundational knowledge to advanced technical expertise. The programme emphasizes: Practical, hands-on learning Exposure to real client projects Continuous mentorship Career readiness for global tech roles Participants are trained to meet international delivery standards , ensuring they are competitive in both local and global job markets. Specializations Offered The programme offers a diverse range of in-demand tech specializations, enabling candidates to choose a path aligned with their interests and career goals: Data Engineering Python Backend + AI Application Development Java Backend Development Quality Assurance (Test Automation) JavaScript Full Stack Development DevOps Engineering Each specialization is designed to provide industry-relevant skills and practical experience with modern tools and technologies. Programme Structure The apprenticeship follows a structured progression through different levels: Junior Associate Level – Foundational training and skill-building Mid-Level Entry – Advanced technical development and project exposure This tiered approach ensures continuous learning and measurable growth throughout the programme. 🎯 Why Choose the AmaliTech Apprenticeship? The programme stands out due to its strong focus on practical experience and career outcomes . Key benefits include: Hands-on Experience Participants work on real international client projects , gaining valuable industry exposure. Mentorship Guidance from experienced professionals and international experts helps accelerate learning and career growth. Soft Skills Training Training in communication, teamwork, and professional development is delivered in collaboration with global partners such as Harambee and EF Education First . Career Opportunities Successful graduates receive a standing job offer from AmaliTech , providing a direct pathway into employment. Certifications Access to internationally recognized certifications enhances employability and credibility. Impact Participants contribute to the growth of Rwanda’s technology ecosystem , supporting innovation and digital transformation. What Past Participants Say Graduates of the programme consistently highlight its transformative impact: Improved technical expertise in modern tools such as Angular, Git, Bootstrap, and TypeScript Stronger problem-solving and leadership skills Enhanced confidence through mentorship and global networking Successful transition into professional software engineering roles These testimonials reflect the programme’s ability to bridge the gap between learning and employment . Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the programme, applicants must: Be currently based in Rwanda or willing to relocate Commit to full-time participation throughout the programme Demonstrate a strong passion for software development and technology careers Show the ability to work independently with initiative and accountability Possess a growth mindset and willingness to learn continuously Have strong problem-solving, teamwork, and communication skills Be fluent in English (spoken and written) Application Process The selection process is designed to identify motivated and capable candidates through four key stages: Screening and Shortlisting Applications are reviewed based on eligibility criteria. In-Person Assessment Candidates complete a computer-based test evaluating programming skills. Interviews Shortlisted candidates participate in face-to-face or online interviews. Offer and Hiring Successful candidates receive an offer to join the programme. Why This Programme Matters The AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programme plays a vital role in: Developing Africa’s digital talent pipeline Bridging the skills gap in the global tech industry Creating employment opportunities for young professionals Supporting innovation and economic growth It is a powerful opportunity for aspiring developers and engineers to build sustainable, future-proof careers in technology . For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programme 2026: Launch Your Career in Software Development and Data Engineering appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UK Youth Fund 2026: £10 Million Grant Supporting Small Youth Organisations Across the United Kingdom
summary: The UK Youth , in partnership with the Pears Foundation , has launched the UK Youth Fund , a transformative £10 million funding initiative aimed at strengthening small youth organisations across the United Kingdom. This ambitious programme is designed to provide multi-year unrestricted funding combined with targeted capacity-building support , helping grassroots organisations build resilience, sustainability, and long-term impact for young people. What is the UK Youth Fund? The UK Youth Fund is a strategic funding initiative focused on empowering small organisations that deliver vital services to young people. Unlike traditional grants, this fund offers flexible, unrestricted funding , allowing organisations to allocate resources where they are most needed. This approach recognizes that grassroots organisations are best positioned to understand their own challenges and opportunities, enabling them to: Strengthen core operations Invest in innovation Improve long-term sustainability Expand their impact on young people The fund builds on a successful partnership between UK Youth and Pears Foundation, which has already delivered impactful programmes such as the COVID Relief Fund (£2.2 million) and the Cost-of-Living Fund (£7 million) . Objectives of the Fund The UK Youth Fund is structured around two key outcomes: 1. Improved Financial Sustainability Through multi-year unrestricted funding, organisations can: Plan strategically for long-term growth Stabilize operations Allocate resources flexibly 2. Increased Capability and Organisational Resilience Through capacity-building support, organisations can: Strengthen internal systems Improve leadership and governance Enhance service delivery for young people Funding Details Eligible organisations can apply for multi-year unrestricted funding of up to: 10% of their annual turnover This proportional funding model ensures: Fair distribution of resources Sustainability aligned with organisational size Long-term financial resilience Organisations are encouraged to use the funding in ways that maximize impact, whether through: Operational strengthening Programme development Innovation initiatives Who Can Apply? The fund is specifically designed for small youth organisations operating in the UK. Eligibility criteria include: Annual turnover of less than £500,000 Delivery of services to young people Operations within eligible communities across: England Scotland Wales Northern Ireland Importantly, eligibility is based on the communities served , not the organisation’s headquarters. Even organisations supporting just one young person from eligible areas can apply, making the fund highly inclusive and accessible. Application Process The UK Youth Fund uses a two-stage application process designed to be accessible and efficient: Stage 1: Expression of Interest (EOI) Short eligibility check and multiple-choice questions Confirms whether organisations meet criteria Stage 2: Full Application Invited applicants submit detailed proposals Focus on impact, sustainability, and organisational needs This structure reduces unnecessary workload and ensures only eligible organisations proceed. Key Dates Expression of Interest opens: April 14, 2026 (2:00 PM) EOI Deadline: May 6, 2026 (11:59 PM) Application Invitation: May 11, 2026 Final Application Deadline: June 3, 2026 (11:59 PM) Applications must be submitted via the official online grant system. Information Webinar Applicants can attend an online information session on April 21, 2026 (2:00 PM) to: Learn more about the fund Understand eligibility criteria Ask questions directly to the grants team Why This Fund Matters The UK Youth Fund arrives at a critical time when youth organisations face increasing demand and financial pressure. By offering flexible, long-term funding , the programme enables organisations to: Provide stable and safe spaces for young people Address social and economic challenges Deliver impactful youth services Build sustainable futures for their communities This initiative represents a major investment in the future of youth work across the UK , ensuring that grassroots organisations can continue transforming young lives. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post UK Youth Fund 2026: £10 Million Grant Supporting Small Youth Organisations Across the United Kingdom appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026: Call for Mentors to Shape Europe’s Next Generation of Women Innovators
summary: The European Innovation Council (EIC) has officially launched its 2026 Call for Mentors under the highly impactful EIC Women Leadership Programme , inviting experienced professionals across Europe’s innovation ecosystem to contribute to the development of high-potential women researchers and entrepreneurs. Running from May to November 2026 , this prestigious initiative offers a unique opportunity for leaders to actively shape the future of European innovation while expanding their own influence, visibility, and professional network. What is the EIC Women Leadership Programme? The EIC Women Leadership Programme is a flagship initiative by the European Innovation Council aimed at empowering women innovators by strengthening their leadership skills, expanding their networks, and supporting their entrepreneurial growth. Since its launch in 2021, the programme has successfully supported over 300 women researchers and entrepreneurs , creating a dynamic and inclusive ecosystem where innovation thrives through diversity and collaboration. A central pillar of the programme is its structured mentoring scheme , which connects: Experienced leaders (mentors) High-potential women innovators (mentees) This exchange fosters meaningful collaboration, knowledge transfer, and long-term professional relationships. Purpose of the Mentor Call The 2026 call seeks highly experienced professionals willing to contribute their expertise on a pro bono basis while engaging in a high-level European innovation network. Mentors play a critical role in: Guiding mentees through leadership challenges Supporting business and venture growth Enhancing strategic thinking and decision-making Expanding mentees’ professional networks Through this engagement, mentors directly contribute to building a stronger, more inclusive European innovation landscape . What’s in It for Mentors? Participating as a mentor offers both professional recognition and strategic opportunities , including: Official mentor recognition and certification Visibility across EIC communication channels (including mentor showcases) Access to an exclusive European network of investors, founders, and innovation leaders Invitations to speaking engagements and thought leadership opportunities Exposure to cutting-edge technologies and breakthrough innovations Participation in prestigious events such as the EIC Summit Opportunity to shape the future of women’s leadership in innovation This is not just a mentoring role—it is a strategic leadership position within Europe’s innovation ecosystem . The Role of a Mentor Mentors are matched with participants based on their expertise and the mentees’ development needs, ensuring a high-impact and personalized mentoring relationship . Key areas of focus include: Leadership development and confident decision-making Strategic thinking and business growth Career advancement and leadership positioning Network expansion and visibility Mentors are expected to share insights, provide guidance, and open doors to opportunities that can accelerate the mentees’ professional journeys. Programme Timeline The mentoring journey is structured to ensure meaningful engagement while respecting mentors’ time: 13 May 2026 – Online onboarding workshop 30 June 2026 – Mentor–mentee matching confirmed July – November 2026 – Mentoring sessions and networking activities 27 November 2026 – Final event, Demo Day, and pitching session (online) Time Commitment The programme is designed to be flexible and manageable: 5–6 online mentoring sessions Each session lasts approximately 1 hour Scheduling is flexible based on mentor availability Who Should Apply? The programme is open to experienced professionals from across Europe’s innovation ecosystem, including: Established entrepreneurs and founders Investors and venture capital professionals C-level executives and senior managers Innovation experts and ecosystem leaders Ideal candidates demonstrate: Strong leadership experience Deep understanding of innovation and entrepreneurship Commitment to supporting the next generation of women leaders Application Process Applications must be submitted through the official EIC application platform , where candidates can provide details about their expertise, experience, and motivation to join the programme. After submission: Applications undergo a selection process Selected mentors are invited to onboarding Matching with mentees is conducted based on expertise and needs Deadline All applications must be submitted by: April 30, 2026 Late applications will not be considered, so early submission is strongly recommended. Why This Opportunity Matters The EIC Women Leadership Programme represents a powerful step toward: Promoting gender equality in innovation Supporting women in leadership and entrepreneurship Building inclusive and diverse innovation ecosystems By becoming a mentor, you are not only giving back—you are actively shaping the future of European innovation leadership . For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026: Call for Mentors to Shape Europe’s Next Generation of Women Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunities in Europe for Early-Career Academics from Africa, Latin America, and the European Neighbourhood
summary: The prestigious Coimbra Group has officially launched its 2026 Scholarship Programme , offering a unique and highly competitive opportunity for early-career professors and researchers from Africa, Latin America, and the European Neighbourhood to advance their academic careers through international collaboration. This initiative stands as one of Europe’s most recognized academic mobility programs, designed to strengthen global research partnerships and promote academic excellence across continents. What is the Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme? The Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme is an annual initiative led by a network of leading European universities. These institutions are known for their long-standing academic traditions, multidisciplinary excellence, and commitment to fostering international cooperation. For 2026, the programme offers 74 fully supported scholarships , enabling selected candidates to undertake short-term research visits at one of 21 top European universities that are members of the Coimbra Group. These scholarships are specifically designed for: Early-career academics University staff engaged in teaching and research Scholars seeking international exposure and collaboration Objectives of the Programme The Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme goes beyond financial support—it is a strategic academic initiative with multiple objectives: Internationalization of Research : Scholars can expand the global reach of their work by collaborating with European institutions. Academic Networking : Participants build lasting partnerships with leading researchers and institutions. Knowledge Exchange : The programme fosters cross-cultural academic dialogue and innovation. Career Advancement : Exposure to European research environments strengthens academic profiles and future opportunities. Host Institutions Participants will conduct their research at one of the Coimbra Group’s 21 member universities , located across Europe. These institutions are recognized for: High academic standards Cutting-edge research facilities Strong international collaboration networks Each university offers a unique research environment, allowing scholars to align their projects with institutional expertise. What Does the Scholarship Cover? While specific funding details may vary depending on the host university, the scholarship typically includes: Financial support for the duration of the research stay Access to university research facilities and resources Opportunities to collaborate with leading European academics Academic mentorship and supervision The programme is designed to reduce financial barriers and allow scholars to fully focus on their research and collaboration goals. Application Process Applications must be submitted online via the official Coimbra Group website , where candidates can: Select their preferred host university Review eligibility requirements Access detailed application guidelines Upload required documents Applicants are strongly encouraged to carefully review the specific requirements of each participating university before applying. Deadline All applications must be submitted by: May 10th, 2026 (midnight CEST) Late submissions will not be considered, so applicants should plan ahead and ensure all documents are completed and uploaded on time. Why You Should Apply This programme represents a rare opportunity to: Conduct research in Europe without long-term relocation Gain international academic exposure Strengthen your research impact and visibility Join a global network of scholars and institutions For early-career researchers aiming to elevate their academic journey, the Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme 2026 is a powerful stepping stone toward global recognition and collaboration. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunities in Europe for Early-Career Academics from Africa, Latin America, and the European Neighbourhood appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: New Opportunities at the Danish Institute for Human Rights – International Activities
summary: If you are seeking a high-impact international career in European affairs, human rights, and public engagement, this EU Programme Officer role presents a compelling opportunity. Hosted by the Danish Institute for Human Rights, this position focuses on the Citizens, Equality, Rights and Values (CERV) Programme—one of the European Union’s flagship funding instruments supporting democracy, equality, and civic participation across Europe. This role is ideal for professionals with strong communication skills, a background in political science or humanities, and a passion for EU programmes, stakeholder engagement, and fundraising. Based in Copenhagen, Denmark, the job offers both national and international exposure, making it a strategic career move for those aiming to work at the intersection of policy, rights, and EU funding mechanisms. 1. EU Engagement CERV Programme Officer The EU Engagement CERV Programme Officer will play a central role in implementing Denmark’s National Contact Point mandate for the EU CERV Programme. The position combines communication, outreach, policy interpretation, and fundraising support within an internationally respected human rights institution. You will act as a bridge between EU funding structures and Danish stakeholders, helping organizations understand, access, and benefit from EU opportunities related to equality, rights, and civic engagement. About the CERV Programme The Citizens, Equality, Rights and Values (CERV) Programme (2021–2027) is an EU initiative designed to: Promote citizens engagement and participation in democratic life Advance equality and combat discrimination Protect and promote fundamental rights Strengthen civil society across EU member states As part of the Danish National Contact Point, you will help organizations navigate this programme, ensuring they can successfully apply for and implement EU-funded projects. About the Danish Institute for Human Rights The Danish Institute for Human Rights is Denmark’s national human rights institution, operating independently under a legal mandate. It plays a critical global role in promoting and implementing human rights standards across governments, NGOs, and private sector actors. The Institute is especially recognized for: Driving sustainable, long-term human rights solutions Collaborating across sectors (public, private, and civil society) Working at the intersection of business, law, and international policy Key Responsibilities 1. Communication Outreach Strategy Lead communication efforts for the CERV programme across Denmark Manage online platforms, publications, and awareness campaigns Organize and participate in national events, including Folkemødet 2. EU Programme Advisory Interpret EU funding priorities and technical requirements Guide Danish stakeholders on funding opportunities and compliance Translate complex EU frameworks into accessible information 3. Thematic Expertise Provide insights on: Equality and non-discrimination Civic participation Fundamental rights and EU values 4. Public Engagement Deliver presentations, seminars, and workshops Represent the programme at national and EU-level events 5. Fundraising Strategic Development Support donor mapping and screening Develop donor profiles and external relations data Contribute to institutional fundraising strategies 6. Project Implementation Assist in planning and executing EU-funded projects Ensure compliance with EU financial and operational requirements Required Qualifications Academic Requirements Master’s degree in: Political Science Humanities Or a related field Professional Experience Background in: Communication Editorial work Public outreach Core Skills Excellent written and verbal communication in: English Danish Strong digital and communication tools proficiency High level of organization and ability to work independently Knowledge Interests Demonstrated interest in: European Union affairs Human rights and equality Civic participation and democracy Willingness to learn EU funding systems and compliance frameworks Eligibility Criteria: Who Can Apply? Category of Applicants This opportunity is best suited for: Early to mid-career professionals EU affairs specialists Human rights practitioners Communication and outreach experts Policy and governance professionals Geographic Eligibility Primarily open to candidates eligible to work in Denmark Strong requirement for Danish language proficiency suggests preference for: Danish nationals EU/EEA candidates with Danish fluency International applicants may be considered if they meet language and work authorization requirements Job Location and Terms Location: Christianshavn, Copenhagen, Denmark Employment Type: Full-time (37 hours per week) Start Date: 1 June 2026 or as soon as possible Salary: Based on Danish Ministry of Finance agreements, with potential for negotiation Why This Opportunity Stands Out Direct involvement with EU funding programmes Strong career pathway into international policy and human rights work Opportunity to influence civic participation and equality initiatives Exposure to high-level stakeholders across government, NGOs, and EU institutions Work within a globally respected human rights institution Application Process Required Documents Applicants must submit: Cover letter (in English) Curriculum Vitae (CV) Academic diplomas Academic transcripts/grades Submission Details Applications must be submitted via the official application portal Deadline: 22 April 2026 at 12:00 (GMT+1) Interview Timeline Interviews are expected to take place during the last week of April 2026 Apply here Deadline 22nd April 2026 Equal Opportunity Statement The Danish Institute for Human Rights is committed to diversity and inclusion. All qualified candidates are encouraged to apply regardless of background. This EU Programme Officer role is more than just a job—it is a gateway into the world of European funding, international development, and human rights advocacy. With its blend of communication, policy, and strategic engagement, it offers a unique platform to make meaningful contributions at both national and European levels. 2. Internship in Human Rights Business Tech If you are passionate about human rights, business ethics, and the intersection of technology and global development, the Internship in Human Rights, Business and Tech – Fall 2026 at the Danish Institute for Human Rights offers an exceptional opportunity to gain hands-on international experience. This in-depth guide provides everything you need to know—from eligibility and responsibilities to application strategy and deadlines. Internship Focus: Human Rights, Business, and Technology This internship is based within the Human Rights, Business and Tech Department, where selected interns will actively contribute to integrating human rights standards into global business practices. A key component of the role is exposure to the UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights, the leading international framework guiding corporate responsibility in human rights. What You Will Do (Roles and Responsibilities) Interns will be fully integrated into project teams and supervised throughout the internship. Your work will be divided into two major areas: 1. Research and Analytical Work (70–80%) You will engage in high-level research across multiple domains, including: Human rights issues in sectors such as: Renewable energy Technology Finance Gender and business-related human rights concerns Country-specific human rights challenges Corporate human rights due diligence practices Legal and policy frameworks: National Action Plans on Business and Human Rights International regulatory standards 2. Administrative and Operational Support (20–30%) You will also support organizational functions such as: Coordinating meetings and international events Archiving documents and managing communications Proofreading and referencing research outputs Drafting reports and taking meeting minutes Assisting in translation work where applicable Skills and Competencies You Will Gain This internship is designed to build both academic and professional capabilities: Advanced research and policy analysis skills Practical application of human rights frameworks Experience in international, multicultural work environments Communication and reporting expertise Time management and prioritization in professional settings Eligibility Criteria: Who Can Apply? Academic Requirements Applicants must: Hold a Bachelor’s degree (BA) in: Human Rights Law Political Science Development Studies Or a related field Be currently enrolled in a Master’s degree program at the time of the internship Be able to transfer the internship into academic credits Professional and Technical Skills Ideal candidates should have: Strong academic or practical interest in human rights or sustainable development Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Excellent written and verbal communication skills Fluency in English (mandatory) Additional language advantages: Spanish French Danish Additional Preferred Experience Exposure to business environments or corporate responsibility Experience with NGOs, development organizations, or human rights initiatives Participation in extracurricular or volunteer activities related to social justice Eligible Applicants by Region This opportunity is open globally, meaning applicants can come from: Africa Europe Asia North America South America Oceania However, international applicants must: Be able to obtain a Danish work permit Secure external funding or scholarships to cover living costs (since the internship is unpaid) Important Note on Funding This internship is unpaid, which is standard in Denmark where domestic students often receive government stipends. International applicants are strongly encouraged to: Apply for scholarships or grants Seek university funding support Explore external fellowship opportunities Work Environment and Benefits While unpaid, the internship offers significant non-financial benefits: Work in a globally respected human rights institution Gain exposure to international policy and corporate responsibility Build a strong professional network Collaborate with experts across sectors and regions Develop career-relevant, real-world experience Application Process To apply, candidates must submit: A CV (Curriculum Vitae) A Cover Letter, clearly outlining: Your motivation Relevant experience Availability (start and end dates) Any academic obligations during the internship Applications must be submitted via the official application portal on the Institute’s website. Apply here Selection Process Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews Interviews will take place within 14 days after the deadline Interviews may be conducted: In person Via Microsoft Teams Deadline 23rd April 2026 This internship is ideal for students seeking a serious, career-defining experience in the field of business and human rights. It is especially valuable for those aiming to work in: International organizations Policy and governance Corporate social responsibility Human rights advocacy Despite being unpaid, the depth of exposure, training, and global relevance makes this one of the most impactful internship opportunities in Europe for 2026. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post New Opportunities at the Danish Institute for Human Rights International Activities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: CIVICUS Global Job Opportunities 2026: High-Paying International NGO Roles
summary: If you are looking for a prestigious international career in human rights, civil society, and global development, opportunities at CIVICUS offer one of the most impactful pathways. This detailed guide explores everything you need to know about CIVICUS job opportunities, including eligibility criteria, qualifications, global accessibility, deadlines, and how to successfully apply. Overview of CIVICUS and Why These Opportunities Matter CIVICUS is a globally recognized international non-profit organization headquartered in Johannesburg, South Africa. Founded in 1993, it works to strengthen citizen action and civil society worldwide, operating in more than 180 countries with over 15,000 members. The organization focuses on: Promoting human rights and civic freedoms Strengthening civil society organizations Supporting activism and citizen participation Amplifying voices from underrepresented regions, especially the Global South Because of its global reach and mission, working with CIVICUS provides exposure to international policy, advocacy, and development work at the highest level. CIVICUS offers multiple employment categories, including: Full-time jobs Consultancy roles Internships Project-based contracts These are professional career opportunities within a global non-profit environment. Current Featured Opportunity Examples 1. Communications Consultants 2. Media Consultants 3. Web Developers 4. Designers Ilustrators 5. Translators Editors 6. Interpreters 7. Videographers Film makers 8. Creative Meeting Facilitators Who Can Apply? (Eligible Categories of Applicants) CIVICUS opportunities are open to a wide range of professionals, including: 1. Early-Career Applicants Recent graduates Entry-level professionals Interns interested in global development 2. Mid-Level Professionals NGO practitioners Communications specialists Project managers Policy analysts 3. Senior Experts Advocacy leaders Human rights specialists Senior consultants and strategists 4. Students and Interns Undergraduate and postgraduate students Individuals seeking hands-on NGO experience Eligible Countries and Regions CIVICUS is a global organization, and its opportunities are generally open to applicants from: Africa Asia Europe North America South America Oceania However, there are important conditions: Many roles are location-specific (e.g., Johannesburg, Geneva, New York) Applicants may need legal authorization to work in the country of employment In some cases, CIVICUS may assist with work permits depending on the role and candidate profile Key Qualifications and Requirements While requirements vary by role, CIVICUS typically looks for the following: Educational Qualifications Bachelor’s degree (minimum requirement for most roles) Master’s degree preferred for senior roles Relevant fields include: International relations Political science Development studies Law Human rights Media and communications Professional Experience Entry-Level / Internships: Basic understanding of NGO or development work Strong communication Familiarity with tools like MS Office and collaboration platforms Mid-Level Roles: 3–6 years of relevant experience Project coordination or communications expertise Experience in civil society or advocacy Senior Roles: 6+ years of experience Leadership or management experience Deep understanding of global governance and human rights systems Core Skills Required Across most roles, CIVICUS emphasizes: Excellent written and spoken English Strong communication and storytelling skills Digital literacy (MS Office, collaboration tools) Ability to work in multicultural, remote teams Project management and organizational skills Knowledge of global civil society trends Commitment to human rights and civic engagement Additional Preferred Skills Knowledge of UN systems and international policy Multilingual ability (French, Spanish, etc.) Experience with advocacy campaigns Media and communications expertise Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide To apply for a CIVICUS opportunity: Step 1: Visit the Official Careers Page Go to the CIVICUS “Work With Us” section to view open vacancies. Step 2: Select a Specific Vacancy CIVICUS does not accept speculative applications. You must apply for a listed position. Step 3: Prepare Required Documents You will need: A detailed CV A one-page cover letter explaining: Your motivation Relevant experience Fit for the role Step 4: Submit Your Application Follow instructions in the job listing (usually via email or application portal). Step 5: Wait for Feedback Expected response time: within 3 weeks after deadline If no response is received, consider the application unsuccessful Apply here Why You Should Apply to CIVICUS Working with CIVICUS offers: Global exposure in international development Opportunities to influence human rights and policy Collaboration with professionals across 180+ countries Remote and flexible work options Career growth in the NGO and international sector Deadline 24th April 2026 CIVICUS job opportunities are among the most competitive and impactful roles in the global civil society ecosystem. Whether you are an entry-level candidate or a seasoned professional, these positions provide a unique platform to contribute to meaningful global change. title: ITR Fellowship 4.0 Application 2026: Fully Funded Virtual Fellowship Empowering Young Africans with Leadership, Skills and Internship Opportunities
summary: The ITR Fellowship 4.0 is a transformative virtual program designed to empower young Africans with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities required to become impactful leaders and change-makers in their communities. This fellowship stands out as a comprehensive personal and professional development initiative aimed at individuals who are at the early stages of their journey and seeking clarity, direction, and capacity-building. With a strong focus on leadership development, social innovation, and real-world impact, the program is structured to guide participants from a place of uncertainty to one of purpose, equipping them with practical tools to create meaningful change. Category of Opportunity Fellowship (with Internship Opportunity) This program falls under the fellowship category, with an added advantage of a post-program internship placement for successful participants. Overview of the ITR Fellowship 4.0 The ITR Fellowship is an 8-week virtual experience tailored specifically for young Africans who are eager to grow personally and contribute to societal development. The fourth cohort introduces an exciting expansion — an internship opportunity for fellows who meet the program’s selection and participation criteria. The fellowship is designed to: Provide clarity and direction for young individuals Build leadership capacity and confidence Equip participants with practical skills for social impact Foster innovation and community engagement Create pathways for mentorship and career development Fellowship Benefits Participants selected for the ITR Fellowship 4.0 will gain access to a wide range of benefits, including: 1. Community Impact Project Fellows will have the opportunity to design and execute a project that addresses a real issue within their community. This hands-on experience allows participants to apply learned concepts and create measurable impact. 2. Post-Fellowship Mentorship Graduates of the program will receive continued guidance from industry experts. This mentorship helps fellows refine their goals, improve their strategies, and navigate their career or impact journey effectively. 3. Internship Placement Opportunity One of the most valuable additions in this cohort is the internship experience. Fellows who meet the program’s requirements will be matched with organizations where they can: Gain practical work experience Develop professional skills Build a strong portfolio Enhance employability Who Should Apply? This fellowship is specifically targeted at: Young Africans who feel under-resourced in personal development Individuals who lack access to mentorship and skill-building opportunities Aspiring change-makers who want to create impact but do not know where to start Youth from humble backgrounds or underrepresented communities If you are motivated, eager to learn, and committed to making a difference, this fellowship is designed for you. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the ITR Fellowship 4.0, applicants must meet the following requirements: Must be an African (from any country within the continent) Must be between 18 and 30 years old Must have proficiency in speaking and understanding English Must have little or no prior experience in community development Must be available and willing to commit to the full 8-week duration of the fellowship Must maintain at least 85% attendance to qualify for graduation, mentorship, and internship opportunities Must demonstrate strong motivation and willingness to contribute to social change and innovation Program Structure and Expectations The fellowship runs for a period of 8 weeks and is conducted virtually, making it accessible to participants across Africa. During the program, fellows will engage in: Interactive training sessions Personal development workshops Leadership and communication training Project design and execution Networking opportunities with fellow participants Attendance and active participation are critical, as they directly impact eligibility for post-fellowship benefits. Application Instructions Applicants must carefully follow the instructions below: Complete all sections of the application form electronically Submit all responses in English language Provide honest and original answers Important Notice Applicants are explicitly advised not to use AI tools in completing their application. Any applicant found violating this rule will be disqualified. Apply here Testimonials from Previous Fellows Past participants of the ITR Fellowship have shared powerful experiences that highlight the program’s impact: Fellows have reported significant growth in confidence, leadership ability, and financial management skills Many participants have successfully launched community projects and initiatives The fellowship has helped individuals discover their purpose and take actionable steps toward achieving it Networking opportunities have led to collaborations and career advancements These testimonials reinforce the fellowship’s effectiveness in shaping future leaders and change-makers across Africa. Why You Should Apply The ITR Fellowship 4.0 is more than just a training program. It is a life-changing opportunity that offers: Personal clarity and direction Practical leadership experience Access to mentorship and professional networks Real-world project execution A pathway to internship and career growth For young Africans seeking a breakthrough in their personal and professional journey, this fellowship provides a solid foundation to build upon. Deadline 30th April 2026 The ITR Fellowship 4.0 represents a unique opportunity for young Africans to step into leadership, develop essential skills, and create meaningful impact in their communities. With its structured training, mentorship, and internship pathway, the program is designed to produce well-rounded individuals ready to drive change across the continent. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post ITR Fellowship 4.0 Application 2026: Fully Funded Virtual Fellowship Empowering Young Africans with Leadership, Skills and Internship Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: Disability Inclusion Fund 2026 Grant for Disability Justice Projects (Up to $150,000)
summary: Applications are now open for the Disability Inclusion Fund (DIF) 2026 Collaborative Grants Program , offering up to $150,000 over two years to support disability-led organizations advancing justice, inclusion, and cross-movement collaboration. The deadline to apply is May 20, 2026 . Key Dates Application Deadline: May 20, 2026 Time: 8:59 PM PT | 9:59 PM CT | 11:59 PM ET Information Webinar: Held April 15, 2026 (recording available) Notification of Results: By November 20, 2026 About Borealis Philanthropy Borealis Philanthropy is a philanthropic intermediary that supports social justice movements across the United States. Through innovative funding strategies, Borealis invests in leaders, grassroots organizations, and collaborative efforts that drive transformative change—especially in disability justice and inclusion. About the Disability Inclusion Fund (DIF) The Disability Inclusion Fund supports U.S.-based, disability-led organizations working toward a more equitable and accessible society. Backed by major donors including the Presidents’ Council on Disability Inclusion in Philanthropy, DIF prioritizes initiatives that center lived experiences and build collective power across movements. Core Values All funded projects align with DIF’s guiding principles: Participation: Centering those most impacted Intersectionality: Recognizing overlapping identities Radical Inclusion: Removing barriers to access Leadership of the Most Impacted: Prioritizing marginalized voices Cross-Movement Solidarity: Building alliances across sectors Sustainability: Strengthening long-term movement capacity What the DIF Collaborative Grants Fund This opportunity supports joint proposals from at least two partner organizations working together toward shared impact. Eligible Project Areas Include: Disability justice initiatives led by and for disabled communities Cross-movement work (e.g., racial justice, climate, labor, immigration, LGBTQ+ rights) Coalition building and collaborative organizing Narrative change to challenge ableism in culture and policy Arts, media, and storytelling amplifying disabled voices Advocacy, grassroots organizing, and policy change efforts Eligibility Criteria To qualify, applicants must meet the following: Partnership Requirement: At least two collaborating organizations Leadership: At least one organization must be disability-led (50%+ leadership with disabilities) Budget Cap: Each organization must have an annual budget under $3 million Location: Must be U.S.-based or U.S. territory-based (or fiscally sponsored) Project Focus: Must align with disability justice and collaborative impact Additional Notes: Partnerships may include universities, individuals, hospitals, or LLCs However, the lead applicant must have a disability-centered mission Projects primarily focused on research or direct service delivery are not eligible Funding Details Grant Size: Up to $75,000 per year Duration: 2 years Total Funding: Up to $150,000 Limit: Cannot exceed 25% of your organization’s total budget Application Process How to Apply Applicants must complete the online eligibility quiz and application form via Borealis Philanthropy’s portal. Important Steps: Choose a Lead Applicant Organization This entity will receive and manage the funds Develop a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) Required for all collaborating partners Complete and Submit the Application Ensure all required fields are filled before submission Saving Your Application Applicants can save progress and return later using the platform’s “Save my progress” feature. Accessibility Inclusive Application Options DIF is committed to accessibility. Alternative formats are available, including: Zoom or phone submissions Word or PDF formats Captioning and ASL interpretation (for webinars) Use of AI in Applications Applicants may use AI tools for drafting responses. However, DIF emphasizes authenticity and real-world experience —applications should reflect your organization’s actual work, challenges, and impact. What Happens Next? Applications are reviewed through a participatory grantmaking process Final decisions are made by a committee of disability advocates and funders Applicants will be notified by November 20, 2026 Application Questions (Overview) Applicants will respond to short narrative questions (max 250 words each), covering: Collaboration goals and partner roles Use of anti-ableist frameworks Project plans and expected outcomes Challenges and support needs Monitoring and evaluation strategies Required Documents Project budget (estimated) Combined organizational budget 501(c)(3) documentation or fiscal sponsorship agreement List of current funders Geographic scope of work The Disability Inclusion Fund 2026 presents a powerful opportunity for disability-led organizations to collaborate, scale impact, and drive systemic change. If your work centers inclusion, justice, and collective action, this grant could be a strong fit. Be sure to submit your application before May 20, 2026 . Visit Here , for Frequently Asked Questions. The post Apply Now: Disability Inclusion Fund 2026 Grant for Disability Justice Projects (Up to $150,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
